services.name: axionenterprise.cloud
services.notes: Static institutional landing and WWW alias.

services.name: flow.axionenterprise.cloud
services.notes: Frontend can move first. Data/API layer should be split from current origin.

services.name: api.axionenterprise.cloud
services.notes: Good candidate for edge API gateway, webhook ingress, and lightweight handlers.

services.name: admin.axionenterprise.cloud
services.notes: Panel frontend and BFF pattern fit serverless well.

services.name: scan.axionenterprise.cloud
services.current: Python scanner coupled to Docker runtime
services.target: Workers/Pages control plane plus redesigned runtime
services.notes: Current implementation depends on Docker socket and container introspection.

services.name: postgres
services.notes: Primary relational state should move here unless specific vector scale dictates otherwise.

services.name: valkey
services.target: Upstash Redis or Cloudflare KV/Durable Objects
services.notes: Choose target per usage pattern: cache, locks, ephemeral state, or config.

services.name: nats
services.notes: Async processing should leave the VPS early.

services.name: grafana/loki/alloy
services.notes: Low strategic value to self-host during migration.

services.name: uptime-kuma
services.notes: External monitoring is more useful during disaster recovery.

services.name: picoclaw-official
services.current: Long-running custom container with persistent workspace
services.notes: Not a clean fit for edge/serverless today.

services.name: tenant-picoclaw-*
services.current: Per-tenant long-running containers with local data
services.notes: Control plane can move serverless before tenant runtime does.

services.name: openclaw-gateway
services.notes: Persistent workspace and process lifecycle make pure serverless unsuitable.
